title: Get a permanent resident card:  Get, renew or replace a permanent resident card - Canada.ca
content:
Get a permanent resident card

## Get, renew or replace a permanent resident card

## If you’re not already a permanent resident
You must first apply for permanent residence and be approved before you can get a PR card.
Explore our immigration programs.

## Get a PR card for the first time (within 180 days of receiving your PR status)
Most people don’t need to apply for their first PR card. We automatically send you your first PR card if you give us your Canadian mailing address and photo within 180 days of becoming a permanent resident.

### Give us your address within the first 180 days of becoming a permanent resident
The way to give us your information depends on how you confirm your permanent resident status.

#### If you confirm your status at a port of entry
You can give your address to a Canada Border Services Agency officer at the port of entry. If you don’t do so when you land in Canada, you can tell us online within 180 days of becoming a permanent resident.
Tell us your address online .
If you can’t give us your address using our online service, use the web form instead. Follow the instructions and include all the details we ask for.
We’ll use the photo attached to the confirmation of permanent residence for your PR card. If your photo does not meet specifications , we’ll contact you and let you know how to provide a new photo.

#### If you’re in Canada and confirm your status virtually
You need to give your Canadian address and upload a digital photo when you confirm your permanent residence in the Permanent Residence Portal.
- Confirm your permanent residence and give us your address .
- Check the photo specifications .

#### If your address changes before you get your PR card
Change your address if you’re moving within 3 weeks or have already moved.

## Get a PR card for the first time (more than 180 days after receiving your PR status)
Most people don’t need to apply for their first PR card. We automatically send you your first PR card if you give us your Canadian mailing address and photo within 180 days of becoming a permanent resident.
If you were not able to get us your photo and address within 180 days, you need to apply for a PR card.
- Start your application in the Permanent Residence Portal In the portal, begin your application by filling out the application for a permanent resident card (IMM 5444). If you need help applying You can get help with technical issues (like photo issues) get alternate formats, like braille or large print check our instruction guide (IMM 5445) Sign in to the Permanent Residence Portal Urgent processing Urgent processing is available in some circumstances. However, the minimum processing time is 3 weeks. Even if you qualify for urgent processing, we can’t guarantee you’ll get your card on time. Apply for urgent processing

### Start your application in the Permanent Residence Portal
In the portal, begin your application by filling out the application for a permanent resident card (IMM 5444).

#### If you need help applying
- get help with technical issues (like photo issues)
- get alternate formats, like braille or large print
- check our instruction guide (IMM 5445)
Sign in to the Permanent Residence Portal
Urgent processing is available in some circumstances. However, the minimum processing time is 3 weeks.
Even if you qualify for urgent processing, we can’t guarantee you’ll get your card on time.
Apply for urgent processing
- Complete additional forms, and upload them to the portal Complete the Document checklist (IMM 5644) , and upload it to the portal. Depending on your circumstances, you may need to download, complete and upload other forms, or provide other documents to support your application. If you’re using a representative Fill out the following form if a paid or unpaid representative is helping you with your application: Use of a representative (IMM 5476) If you want someone else to access your information Fill out the following form to allow someone who’s not a representative to access information about your application. Authority to Release Personal Information to a Designated Individual (IMM 5475)

### Complete additional forms, and upload them to the portal
Complete the Document checklist (IMM 5644) , and upload it to the portal.
Depending on your circumstances, you may need to download, complete and upload other forms, or provide other documents to support your application.
Fill out the following form if a paid or unpaid representative is helping you with your application:
- Use of a representative (IMM 5476)
Fill out the following form to allow someone who’s not a representative to access information about your application.
- Authority to Release Personal Information to a Designated Individual (IMM 5475)
- Pay your fees, then upload the receipt to the portal Fees are not paid through the Permanent Residence Portal. Pay your fee to IRCC online , then
        upload your receipt to the Fee Payment section in the Permanent Residence Portal to show that you have
        paid. Refunds are not possible while the application is in progress.

### Pay your fees, then upload the receipt to the portal
Fees are not paid through the Permanent Residence Portal.
Pay your fee to IRCC online , then
        upload your receipt to the Fee Payment section in the Permanent Residence Portal to show that you have
        paid.
Refunds are not possible while the application is in progress.
- Review your application, then submit Before you submit your application, make sure you answer all the questions sign your application and all forms electronically (typing your full name exactly as shown on your
          passport) include your processing fee receipt upload all the supporting documents

### Review your application, then submit
Before you submit your application, make sure you
- answer all the questions
- sign your application and all forms electronically (typing your full name exactly as shown on your
          passport)
- include your processing fee receipt
- upload all the supporting documents
- Get updates about your application Track the status of your
        application .

### Get updates about your application
Track the status of your
        application .

## Renew an existing PR card
A PR card is usually valid for 5 years. In some cases, it’s valid for only 1 year.
- if it will expire in less than 9 months or
The new card will have a new expiration date.
- Start your application in the Permanent Residence Portal In the portal, begin your application by filling out the application for a permanent resident card (IMM 5444). If you need help applying You can get help with technical issues (like photo issues) get alternate formats, like braille or large print check our instruction guide (IMM 5445) Sign in to the Permanent Residence Portal Urgent processing Urgent processing is available in some circumstances. However, the minimum processing time is 3 weeks. Even if you qualify for urgent processing, we can’t guarantee you’ll get your card on time. Apply for urgent processing

### Start your application in the Permanent Residence Portal
In the portal, begin your application by filling out the application for a permanent resident card (IMM 5444).

#### If you need help applying
- get help with technical issues (like photo issues)
- get alternate formats, like braille or large print
- check our instruction guide (IMM 5445)
Sign in to the Permanent Residence Portal
Urgent processing is available in some circumstances. However, the minimum processing time is 3 weeks.
Even if you qualify for urgent processing, we can’t guarantee you’ll get your card on time.
Apply for urgent processing
- Complete additional forms, and upload them to the portal Complete the Document checklist (IMM 5644) , and upload it to the portal. Depending on your circumstances, you may need to download, complete and upload other forms, or provide other documents to support your application. The forms that may be required, depending on your situation: If you’re using a representative Fill out the following forms if a paid or unpaid representative is helping you with your application: Use of a representative (IMM 5476) If you want someone else to access your information Fill out the following form to allow someone who’s not a representative to access information about your application. Authority to Release Personal Information to a Designated Individual (IMM 5475) If you’re updating the gender identifier on your card Request for a change of sex or gender identifier (IRM 0002) If you’re changing your name on your PR card Find out which documents you need to provide if you’re changing your name due to marriage or divorce reclaiming your Indigenous name changing your name for another reason

### Complete additional forms, and upload them to the portal
Complete the Document checklist (IMM 5644) , and upload it to the portal.
Depending on your circumstances, you may need to download, complete and upload other forms, or provide other documents to support your application.
The forms that may be required, depending on your situation:
Fill out the following forms if a paid or unpaid representative is helping you with your application:
- Use of a representative (IMM 5476)
Fill out the following form to allow someone who’s not a representative to access information about your application.
- Authority to Release Personal Information to a Designated Individual (IMM 5475)
- Request for a change of sex or gender identifier (IRM 0002)
Find out which documents you need to provide if you’re
- changing your name due to marriage or divorce
- reclaiming your Indigenous name
- changing your name for another reason
- Pay your fees, then upload the receipt to the portal Fees are not paid through the Permanent Residence Portal. Pay your fee to IRCC online , then
        upload your receipt to the Fee Payment section in the Permanent Residence Portal to show that you have
        paid. Refunds are not possible while the application is in progress.

### Pay your fees, then upload the receipt to the portal
Fees are not paid through the Permanent Residence Portal.
Pay your fee to IRCC online , then
        upload your receipt to the Fee Payment section in the Permanent Residence Portal to show that you have
        paid.
Refunds are not possible while the application is in progress.
- Review your application, then submit Before you submit your application, make sure you answer all the questions sign your application and all forms electronically (typing your full name exactly as shown on your passport) include your processing fee receipt upload all the supporting documents

### Review your application, then submit
Before you submit your application, make sure you
- answer all the questions
- sign your application and all forms electronically (typing your full name exactly as shown on your passport)
- include your processing fee receipt
- upload all the supporting documents
- Get updates about your application Track the status of your application .

### Get updates about your application
Track the status of your application .

## We made a mistake on your card (and you received your card in the last 60 days)
When we make a mistake or miss something on your card, you can apply to have your card reissued at no cost.
You need to submit the request within 60 days of receiving your card.

### If only part of your name appears on your card
The names on your PR card need to match your foreign passport, but there’s limited space for names on a PR card. We may shorten your name to make it fit on the card.
The card has space for
- 28 letters or less for first names
- 28 letters or less for last names
If we shorten your name, it’s not a mistake. We won’t update your PR card if we had to shorten your name.

### Get your card reissued
- Complete the application Fill out the Request to Reissue a Permanent Resident Card (IMM 5531) (opens in a new tab) . Need help? Our instruction guide has detailed information on how to prepare, complete and submit your application.

#### Complete the application
Fill out the Request to Reissue a Permanent Resident Card (IMM 5531) (opens in a new tab) .
Need help? Our instruction guide has detailed information on how to prepare, complete and submit your application.
- Complete additional forms, and include supporting documents Provide the following form and supporting documents with your application: a photo of the card containing an error a copy of your Record of Landing (IMM 1000) or Confirmation of Permanent Residence a copy of the identity page of your passport 2 photos, if your photo is the reason the card needs to be reissued If you requested a change to one of your permanent resident documents Be sure to include a copy of the approval of your request. This might be a copy of your approved Request to Amend Immigration Record of Landing or Confirmation of Permanent Residence form (IMM 1436) and a copy of the cover letter you received with the approved request or a copy of your Verification of Status If you’re using a representative Fill out the following form if a paid or unpaid representative is helping you with your application: Use of a representative (IMM 5476) If you want someone else to access your information Fill out the following form to allow someone who’s not a representative to access information about your application. Authority to Release Personal Information to a Designated Individual (IMM 5475)

#### Complete additional forms, and include supporting documents
Provide the following form and supporting documents with your application:
- a photo of the card containing an error
- a copy of your Record of Landing (IMM 1000) or Confirmation of Permanent Residence
- a copy of the identity page of your passport
- 2 photos, if your photo is the reason the card needs to be reissued If you requested a change to one of your permanent resident documents Be sure to include a copy of the approval of your request. This might be a copy of your approved Request to Amend Immigration Record of Landing or Confirmation of Permanent Residence form (IMM 1436) and a copy of the cover letter you received with the approved request or a copy of your Verification of Status If you’re using a representative Fill out the following form if a paid or unpaid representative is helping you with your application: Use of a representative (IMM 5476) If you want someone else to access your information Fill out the following form to allow someone who’s not a representative to access information about your application. Authority to Release Personal Information to a Designated Individual (IMM 5475)
Be sure to include a copy of the approval of your request. This might be
- a copy of your approved Request to Amend Immigration Record of Landing or Confirmation of Permanent Residence form (IMM 1436) and a copy of the cover letter you received with the approved request or
- a copy of your Verification of Status
Fill out the following form if a paid or unpaid representative is helping you with your application:
- Use of a representative (IMM 5476)
Fill out the following form to allow someone who’s not a representative to access information about your application.
- Authority to Release Personal Information to a Designated Individual (IMM 5475)
- Review and mail your application Before you submit your application, make sure you answer all the questions include all the required documents sign and date the form Mail your application to the following address: Regular mail PR Card Processing Centre Client Service Unit P.O. Box 9000 Sydney, NS B1P 6K7 Courier IRCC Digitization Centre - PRC 3050 Wilson Ave New Waterford, NS B1H 5V8 On the envelope, print “Urgent — Request to Reissue PR Card” your name and address at the top left-hand side of the envelope

#### Review and mail your application
Before you submit your application, make sure you
- answer all the questions
- include all the required documents
- sign and date the form
Mail your application to the following address:
PR Card Processing Centre Client Service Unit P.O. Box 9000 Sydney, NS B1P 6K7
IRCC Digitization Centre - PRC 3050 Wilson Ave New Waterford, NS B1H 5V8
On the envelope, print
- “Urgent — Request to Reissue PR Card”
- your name and address at the top left-hand side of the envelope
- Get updates about your application Track the status of your
        application .

#### Get updates about your application
Track the status of your
        application .

## We made a mistake on your card (and you received your card more than 60 days ago)
You need to apply for a new card.
- Start your application in the Permanent Residence Portal In the portal, begin your application by filling out the application for a permanent resident card (IMM
        5444). If you need help applying You can get
            help with technical issues (like photo issues) get
            alternate formats, like braille or large print check
            our instruction guide (IMM 5445) Sign in to the Permanent Residence Portal Urgent processing Urgent processing is available in some circumstances. However, the minimum processing time is 3 weeks. Even if you qualify for urgent processing, we can’t guarantee you’ll get your card on time. Apply for urgent processing

#### Start your application in the Permanent Residence Portal
In the portal, begin your application by filling out the application for a permanent resident card (IMM
        5444).

#### If you need help applying

- check

Urgent processing is available in some circumstances. However, the minimum processing time is 3 weeks.
Even if you qualify for urgent processing, we can’t guarantee you’ll get your card on time.
Apply for urgent processing

Depending on your circumstances, you may need to download, complete and upload other forms, or provide other documents to support your application. The forms that may be required, depending on your situation: If you’re using a representative Fill out the following form if a paid or unpaid representative is helping you with your application: Use of a representative (IMM 5476) If you want someone else to access your information Fill out the following form to allow someone who’s not a representative to access information about your application. Authority to release personal information to a designated individual (IMM 5475) If you’re updating the gender identifier on your card Request form for a change of sex or gender identifier (IRM 0002) If you’re changing your name on your PR card Find out which documents you need to provide if you’re changing your name due to marriage or divorce reclaiming your Indigenous name changing your name for another reason

#### Complete additional forms, and upload them to the portal
Complete the Document checklist (IMM 5644) , and upload it to the portal.
Depending on your circumstances, you may need to download, complete and upload other forms, or provide other documents to support your application.
The forms that may be required, depending on your situation:
Fill out the following form if a paid or unpaid representative is helping you with your application:
- Use of a representative (IMM 5476)
Fill out the following form to allow someone who’s not a representative to access information about your application.
- Authority to release personal information to a designated individual (IMM 5475)
- Request form for a change of sex or gender identifier (IRM 0002)
Find out which documents you need to provide if you’re
- changing your name due to marriage or divorce
- reclaiming your Indigenous name
- changing your name for another reason
- Pay your fees, then upload the receipt to the portal Fees are not paid through the Permanent Residence Portal. Pay your fee to IRCC online , then
        upload your receipt to the Fee Payment section in the Permanent Residence Portal to show that you have
        paid. Refunds are not possible while the application is in progress.

#### Pay your fees, then upload the receipt to the portal

Pay your fee to IRCC online , then

Refunds are not possible while the application is in progress.
- Review your application, then submit Before you submit your application, make sure you answer all the questions sign your application and all forms electronically (typing your full name exactly as shown on your
          passport) include your processing fee receipt upload all the supporting documents

#### Review your application, then submit
Before you submit your application, make sure you

- sign your application and all forms electronically (typing your full name exactly as shown on your
          passport)
- include your processing fee receipt

## You made a mistake in your application, or your personal information has changed
You can apply for a new card if you made a mistake in your application or if you want to update your information
    for reasons such as
- a name change after marriage or divorce
- a change of gender identifier
- an updated signature
- Start your application in the Permanent Residence Portal In the portal, begin your application by filling out the application for a permanent resident card (IMM 5444). If you need help applying You can get

            our instruction guide (IMM 5445) Sign in to the Permanent Residence Portal Urgent processing Urgent processing is available in some circumstances. However, the minimum processing time is
          3 weeks. Even if you qualify for urgent processing, we can’t guarantee you’ll get your card on
          time. Apply for urgent processing

## Replace a lost, stolen or damaged PR card (you’re in Canada)
You can apply for a new card.
If you don’t need a new card
If you just want to report your lost or stolen card, and you do not need a new card, you can submit a solemn declaration .
- Start your application in the Permanent Residence Portal In the portal, begin your application by filling out the application for a permanent resident card (IMM
        5444). If you need help applying You can get help with technical issues (like photo issues) get alternate formats, like braille or large print check our instruction guide (IMM 5445) Sign in to the Permanent Residence Portal Urgent processing Urgent processing is available in some circumstances. However, the minimum processing time is 3 weeks. Even if you qualify for urgent processing, we can’t guarantee you’ll get your card on time. Apply for urgent processing

#### Start your application in the Permanent Residence Portal
In the portal, begin your application by filling out the application for a permanent resident card (IMM
        5444).

#### If you need help applying
- get help with technical issues (like photo issues)
- get alternate formats, like braille or large print
- check our instruction guide (IMM 5445)
Sign in to the Permanent Residence Portal
Urgent processing is available in some circumstances. However, the minimum processing time is 3 weeks.
Even if you qualify for urgent processing, we can’t guarantee you’ll get your card on time.
Apply for urgent processing
- Complete additional forms, and upload them to the portal Complete the Document checklist (IMM 5644) , and upload it to the portal. If you’re applying to replace a damaged card , you should destroy your card immediately and upload a photo of your destroyed card as proof that you destroyed it If you lost your PR card, include a copy of a police report or incident number with your application, if you have one. Depending on your circumstances, you may also need to download, complete and upload other forms, or provide other documents to support your application. The forms that may be required, depending on your situation: If you’re using a representative Fill out the following form if a paid or unpaid representative is helping you with your application: Use of a representative (IMM 5476) If you want someone else to access your information Fill out the following form to allow someone who’s not a representative to access information about your application. Authority to Release Personal Information to a Designated Individual (IMM 5475) If you’re updating the gender identifier on your card Request form for a change of sex or gender identifier (IRM 0002) If you’re changing your name on your PR card Find out which documents you need to provide if you’re changing your name due to marriage or divorce reclaiming your Indigenous name changing your name for another reason

#### Complete additional forms, and upload them to the portal
Complete the Document checklist (IMM 5644) , and upload it to the portal.
If you’re applying to replace a damaged card , you should
- destroy your card immediately and
- upload a photo of your destroyed card as proof that you destroyed it
If you lost your PR card, include a copy of a police report or incident number with your application, if you have one.
Depending on your circumstances, you may also need to download, complete and upload other forms, or provide other documents to support your application.
The forms that may be required, depending on your situation:
Fill out the following form if a paid or unpaid representative is helping you with your application:
- Use of a representative (IMM 5476)
Fill out the following form to allow someone who’s not a representative to access information about your application.
- Authority to Release Personal Information to a Designated Individual (IMM 5475)
- Request form for a change of sex or gender identifier (IRM 0002)
Find out which documents you need to provide if you’re
- changing your name due to marriage or divorce
- reclaiming your Indigenous name
- changing your name for another reason
- Pay your fees, then upload the receipt to the portal Fees are not paid through the Permanent Residence Portal. Pay your fee online with IRCC , then
        upload your receipt to the Fee Payment section in the Permanent Residence Portal to show that you have
        paid. Refunds are not possible while the application is in progress.

Refunds are not possible while the application is in progress.

## Apply for a permanent resident travel document
If you don’t have a valid PR card, you must apply for a permanent resident travel document (PRTD) to return to Canada.
If you’re coming back to Canada in a private vehicle , you can use other documents to return to Canada.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
